parts.label: Primary Structure (3U)

parts.label: Solar Panel x 4 (deploy)
parts.spec: Deployable solar panel wing, 3U size, spring-loaded hinge, burn-wire release, deployment damper

parts.label: Cold Gas Thruster x 4

Include PC/104 standoffs and corner posts for structural integrity and mounting

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to extended temperature range.
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion joints and use materials with low coefficient of thermal expansion.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to tight tolerances.
risk.mit: Increase clearance between interfaces slightly to allow for minor alignment errors.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under launch loads.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce critical areas if necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for avionics modules.
risk.mit: Ensure service access panels are easily removable and provide clear instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal cycling damage to electronics.
risk.mit: Use thermal barriers and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may cause mechanical stress on the antenna deployer.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion coefficients in material selection and design.

risk.desc: Incorrect grounding scheme may cause electrical interference.

risk.desc: Inadequate access for maintenance and inspection.

verify.rule: All required fields are present in the output.
The mass estimate is provided and is physically plausible.

risk.desc: The thermal dissipation fins may not be sufficient to handle the heat generated by the thrusters in deep space conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal analysis and consider adding additional cooling mechanisms if necessary.

risk.desc: The plumbing manifold is a single point of failure for the thruster system.
risk.mit: Design redundancy into the plumbing manifold or include a secondary manifold as a backup.

risk.desc: The propellant refill port may be difficult to access during ground operations if the CubeSat is fully assembled.
risk.mit: Ensure the propellant refill port is easily accessible or design the CubeSat to allow access without fully disassembling the satellite.

risk.desc: The mounting bosses may not provide sufficient clamping force to secure the bracket to the CubeSat structure.
risk.mit: Review the clamping force requirements and adjust the mounting boss design or add additional fastening points.

risk.desc: The harness routing path may be prone to damage during deployment and retraction of the thrusters.
risk.mit: Design the harness routing path to avoid areas of high stress and consider adding protective sleeves or conduits.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Cold gas propulsion thruster bracket, 4 thrusters cross-pattern, 50mN class, plumbing manifold

physics_paths.path: Propellant gas from manifold to thruster nozzles
physics_paths.description: Gas flows from the central plumbing manifold to the four thrusters mounted in a cross pattern.

physics_paths.path: Heat dissipation from thrusters to bracket
physics_paths.description: Thermal energy generated by thrusters is dissipated through the bracket to the external environment.
Thrusters can be deployed and retracted for on/off control, with limited motion allowed by the bracket design.

physics_paths.path: Internal pressure within the thruster nozzles
physics_paths.description: Internal pressure within the thruster nozzles is maintained and regulated by the bracket design.
Propellant refill port allows for propellant refill operations.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Attach thruster bracket to payload module first
Secure structure module to thruster bracket
Connect propellant manifold to thruster bracket

integration_opportunities.description: Propellant refill port for easy access during ground operations.
Mounting bosses provide attachment points for securing the bracket to the CubeSat structure.
Thermal dissipation fins enhance heat transfer from the thrusters to the external environment.
